title: Practice Questions Lecture 4 With Answers
body:
Which of the following statement(s) is (are) correct?

                     I.The price of a callable bond is always lower or equal to the price of the
                       equivalent standard bond with same characteristics.
                    II.The price of callable bonds tends to increase when the volatility of interest rates
                       increases.
                   III.A callable bond can be viewed as a portfolio of a standard bond and a
                       repurchase option held by the issuer.
                   IV.A callable bond is a bond that can be repurchased by the investor at the call
                       price.

              a.           I and II.
              b.           I and III.
              c.           I, II, and III.
              d.           All the statements are true.
              e.           None of the above.




Which of the following statement(s) is (are) correct?

              I.The price of a puttable bond is always lower or equal to the equivalent callable
                bond with the same characteristics.
             II.The price of a callable bond is always higher or equal to that of an equivalent
                bond with the same characteristics and issued by the same firm.
            III.The higher the call price, the lower the value of the callable bond.

              a.           I only.
              b.           I and II only.
              c.           III only
              d.           II and III only.
              e.           All statements are wrong.




What does the credit spread of a plain vanilla corporate bond capture?

                      a.   Interest rate risk
                      b.   Currency risk
                      c.   Volatility risk
                      d.   Default risk
 Given the tree below, what is the price of callable bond that matures in 3 years, has a face value of $100,

Question

Before leaving for a week of vacation in Cuba, your colleague at TDAH Asset Management was
working on the valuation of bonds issued by ABC using a tree for spot rates. She left notes on her
desk but did not finish the project on which she worked. For this project, she was responsible to
price callable bond issued by ABC. Your senior manager asks that you take the work where she
left. He asks you to follow the same assumptions that she made. Based on her work, you know that
she assumed that the probability of an upward movement in the tree is equal to the probability of
a downward movement (i.e. p=(1-p)=0.5). However, you are not able to completely read all the
information on her draft. You could successfully read the following:

                                                        𝑟2,𝐻𝐻 = 6.559%

                                𝑟1,𝐻 = 4.595%


      𝑟0 = 2.5%                                          𝑟2,𝐻𝐿 = 4.889%


                                  𝑟1,𝐿 = ? ? ?

                                                            𝑟2,𝐿𝐿 =? ? ?



  a) What volatility parameter has your colleague used in order to build her tree?


We can infer the parameter from r2,LL and r2,HL. We have:
    b) Reproduce and complete the tree with the missing interest rates based on your answer to a)


The missing values in the tree are:




You know that your colleague used the following bond, issued by ABC, to construct her tree. The payments
of this bond are annual and the characteristics are as follows:
Bond ABC
Maturity: 3 years; Face Value: $1,000; Coupon Rate: 4%; Price: $1,005.87; Option: None (neither
callable or puttable)

   c) Do you conclude that the tree built by your colleague is well-calibrated or not? Show detailed
      calculations.
We simply need to price the bonds and see if we obtain the same result.




We thus conclude that the tree is well calibrated.


Please use the tree above to answer the following questions:
The bond that you wish to analyze now is puttable. It has the same characteristics as bond ABC, the only
difference being that this new bond is puttable at $1,000 on the first coupon date only (i.e. ABC can only
buy back the bond on date 1 solely).

   d) What is the price of the puttable bond?
The bond can only be called at time t=1. V1,H becomes 1000 as the bond is sold back to the firm
(instead of 978.60). We have




   e) What is the value of the option embedded in the puttable bond?
The value of the option is the difference between the price of the puttable bond minus the price of
the normal bond:
